chapter_title: Criteria determining eligibility for the School Bus Program
snippet: This section explains the criteria used to determine if a student is eligible to access the School Bus Program (SBP). To access a school bus service, a student must: Students are expected to use public transport where a service is available within 1.6km (measured by shortest practicable route) of their home, or where a service is closer or an equal distance to their home than the nearest school bus service. The final leg of the public transport service should stop within 400 m of the school and the travel time on board the public transport (including interchange time but not including travel time to the public transport) should be no more than 60 minutes. This applies to both eligible and ineligible students. Where school and public transport services align, students must be allocated to public transport services prior to school bus services, as long as this ensures a safe and economically responsible outcome. Students aged under 18 have access to free public transport. More information is available via Journey Planner – Transport Victoria External Link . In making travel allocations the department and the Department of Transport and Planning consider: With some services the demand for seats can exceed the carrying capacity of the bus.
